outlook: Content strategy roles remain steady but stagnant, with 1,094 open positions showing zero growth from last year and a flat demand trend over the past 12 months. The $86,000 median salary reflects the strategic value of the role, while the moderate AI risk score of 41 out of 100 suggests content strategists face meaningful but manageable disruption from tools like Claude and Perplexity. The market is rewarding fundamental human skills like writing, reading comprehension, and active listening over technical capabilities, as declining skills include GUI development software and data mining tools. Content strategists who double down on strategic thinking and human-centered communication will likely weather AI disruption better than those who rely heavily on data analysis and technical content tools.

skillPivots.headline: Master AI-assisted content workflows
skillPivots.body: Since Claude and Perplexity are displacing traditional content research, learn to use these AI tools as collaborative partners for ideation and research while emphasizing your human skills in Active Listening to understand client needs that AI cannot interpret.

skillPivots.headline: Develop strategic reading comprehension expertise
skillPivots.body: Position yourself as a content intelligence specialist by leveraging the rising demand for Reading Comprehension skills to analyze competitor content, market research, and user feedback at scale. This moves you beyond basic writing into strategic content decision-making.

skillPivots.headline: Transition from GUI tools to conversational strategy
skillPivots.body: As demand for graphical interface software declines, pivot your technical skills toward facilitating stakeholder workshops and client consultations, using your strengthened Active Listening abilities to translate business objectives into content strategies.
